summary: Research on benthic marine algae in the tropical and subtropical western Atlantic has been fundamentally reshaped over the past half-century by the integration of gene sequencing technologies, DNA barcoding, and phylogenetic analysis, which have collectively revealed a substantial increase in recognized species diversity. These molecular methods have led to the division of previously unified genera and the discovery of numerous cryptic species, driven by a growing community of phycologists and researchers employing SCUBA for collection. The geographic scope remains focused on the tropical and subtropical western Atlantic, where these advances have significantly revised taxonomic understanding and documented patterns of hidden biodiversity.
